commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=66fc093549c4c6761aa08db5fe8c2a578c8e2072)
commands.review_summary:
The PR passes the detected gateway supervisor into the managed-service update handoff and launches systemd-supervised helpers through `systemd-run --user` with mocked success and failure tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main starts the managed update helper as a detached child under the documented systemd `KillMode=control-group` unit, and the linked report gives concrete Linux/systemd symptoms. I did not run a live systemd update in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual review and contributor action are needed because the patch has concrete systemd-run contract defects and automation cannot supply the missing real Linux systemd proof.

target.title: fix(gateway): escape systemd cgroup for update handoff helper
target.body:
## Summary

The update button does nothing on systemd-supervised installs because the handoff helper is killed by `KillMode=control-group` before it can execute.

## Root Cause

`update-managed-service-handoff.ts:324` spawns the helper with `{ detached: true }`, which creates a new process group but does not escape the systemd unit's cgroup. When the gateway exits for restart, systemd kills all processes in the cgroup — including the helper.

## Fix

When the supervisor is `systemd`, spawn the helper via `systemd-run --user --scope` to place it in a transient scope unit outside the gateway's cgroup. Non-systemd paths (launchd, Windows, unsupervised) are unchanged. `KillMode=control-group` is intentionally preserved for normal child process cleanup.

If `systemd-run` fails (ENOENT, no user bus), the handoff throws immediately rather than falling back to a doomed detached spawn.

## Tests

3 new tests: systemd path uses `systemd-run`, non-systemd uses plain detached spawn, `systemd-run` failure throws. All existing update tests pass.

Fixes #84068

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On systemd-supervised installs, the update handoff helper is now spawned via `systemd-run --user --scope` to escape the gateway's cgroup, preventing `KillMode=control-group` from killing it during restart.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `66fc093549`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: The test file `src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts` was authored as part of this PR. On upstream/main (`a002c416c7`), the production code always uses `{ detached: true }` without `systemd-run`, so the 3 new tests (systemd path, non-systemd path, systemd-run failure) would fail.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
 ✓ gateway-client ../../src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (8 tests) 615ms
 ✓ gateway-methods ../../src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (8 tests) 599ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Duration  1.74s (transform 480ms, setup 305ms, import 77ms, tests 1.21s, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 16 update handoff tests pass across both gateway-client and gateway-methods projects, including the 3 new systemd-specific tests.
- **What was not tested**: Live systemd environment was not tested (macOS has no systemd). The proof uses mocked `child_process.spawn` calls to verify the correct arguments (`systemd-run --user --scope --`) are passed when the supervisor is detected as systemd.
target.author: jerry-xin
